items.title: 'Guesswork out of grooming': expert warning over student photos on social media

children.content: The independent girls' school in Brisbane is now only sharing what it says are unidentifiable shots of students' faces from the side or behind, due to concerns about image manipulation.

gallery.altText: Unidentifiable photos of students in green uniforms.

thumbnail.altText: Unidentifiable photos of students in green uniforms.

items.title: Survivors of institutional abuse in Queensland want this loophole closed

image.altText: A sepia photo of a young girl

children.content: Survivors of institutional abuse are calling on the state to follow other states in closing a legal loophole stopping victims from seeking justice.

items.title: Crime scene declared after body found in burning car west of the Gold Coast

children.content: Emergency services were called to the scene just before 6am on Sunday morning where a body was found in a burning car.

image.altText: An aerial shot of a burned out car on the side of a road alongside a fire engine

items.title: Cowboy chases dream to make his board game an Aussie classic

children.content: Steve Hawe says he never set out to make a board game — but when inspiration struck, he couldn't ignore it.

image.altText: Steve Hawe sitting in front of hand drawn board game holding a little horse piece and smiling.

items.title: Protesters arrested at pro-Palestine rally in Brisbane

children.content: Protesters have been arrested for chanting and displaying a phrase banned in Queensland at a rally in Brisbane on Saturday.

image.altText: A woman in white being arrested

items.title: Search underway for man missing from cruise ship off Brisbane

children.content: Carnival Cruise Line has confirmed it is assisting authorities in a search for a missing man.

items.title: Women who waited years for diagnosis call for more education about FND

children.content: People with functional neurological disorder say they experience misdiagnosis and struggles within the medical system.

gallery.altText: A woman stands smiling and holding her arms out in front of large red rocks.

thumbnail.altText: A woman stands smiling and holding her arms out in front of large red rocks.

items.title: How Maltese migrants united to build a Queensland sugarcane legacy

children.content: Long before they were cast in bronze, a group of Maltese migrants quietly reshaped who would own farmland across north Queensland.

gallery.altText: A closeup of the face of a bronze statue of a man

thumbnail.altText: A closeup of the face of a bronze statue of a man

avatar.alt: A lady looks at the camera, smiling.

items.title: Queensland government in discussions for new oil refinery

children.content: As the fuel crisis continues, a proposal for an $11 billion new oil refinery in central Queensland has been put forward.

image.altText: A close up of the exhaust pipes at a petrol refinery.

items.title: Daughter tells jury she saw mum crush drugs into lethal smoothie

children.content: The daughter of a Gold Coast woman accused of killing her son alleges she saw her mum use a hammer to crush up drugs before putting them into a lethal fruit smoothie.

image.altText: A woman with sunglasses and wearing a pink and white shirt.

items.title: Unlicensed residential care providers in Qld given millions in funding, inquiry hears

children.content: A commission of inquiry was launched last year to review and fix systemic issues that allowed the sector to deteriorate, leading to inadequate outcomes for vulnerable children.

thumbnail.altText: Two anonymous primary school-aged boys walk and balance on playground equipment in a park.

avatar.alt: A woman with cropped red hair, smiling.

items.title: The month-long preparations behind an act of Anzac Day remembrance

children.content: A remembrance tradition that Australians have adapted to commemorate fallen soldiers has ancient Greek and Roman origins.

image.altText: woman holding australian native floral wreath inside a room filled with flowers

items.title: End of school holidays sees sunshine in south-east Queensland, rainfall in north

children.content: The south-east corner will see slightly warmer temperatures and sunshine until Sunday, however, localised heavy falls are on the cards for parts of the central interior and north-west.

image.altText: Drone shot of houses spread along a hill before an oceanfront with a mountain the background

items.title: By-election called one week after Qld state MP's death

children.content: Former Member for Stafford Jimmy Sullivan was found dead at his home earlier this month, after police were called to reports of a non-suspicious death.

image.altText: A man in a white shirt in front of microphones

items.title: Coca-Cola flags it could pull out of Qld recycling scheme

children.content: The Queensland government recently introduced proposed laws to overhaul the governance of the scheme, while also ruling out an increase to the 10-cent refund amount.

image.altText: Coke cans at a WA containers for change depot

items.title: Companies and directors fined $7m over payday lending scheme

children.content: The fines were issued against an operation that breached credit rules and generated more than $91 million in fees and charges.

gallery.altText: Hand holding a mobile phone with the Cigno app showing words: Why choose Cigno?

thumbnail.altText: Hand holding a mobile phone with the Cigno app showing words: Why choose Cigno?

items.title: Elderly couple beg council to let them stay in Gold Coast caravan park

children.content: Time is running out for residents facing eviction from Kirra Beach Tourist Park. In 2018, the council told them they needed to move out by 2028 to make way for tourists, but with rising rent and property prices, they say they cannot afford to live anywhere else.

image.altText: man and woman with dog sitting on lap

items.title: Queensland massage therapist faces 18 sexual assault charges

children.content: A Queensland massage therapist arrested in February over the alleged sexual assault of four clients is facing 11 fresh charges after another four female clients came forward.

image.altText: A man with a receding hairline and rough stubble smiles outdoors.

items.title: Next-gen cricket talent shines at national Indigenous championships

children.content: The National Indigenous Cricket Championships, held in Mackay for the second year, has become one of the key development pathways for young talent.

gallery.altText: A woman wearing cricket clothes looking down at the camera

items.title: Son charged with mother's murder in Far North Queensland

children.content: The woman's body was found at an address in Mareeba in Far North Queensland.

items.title: CFMEU leaders had 'the ear' of former state government, inquiry hears

children.content: Former deputy director-general Peter McKay told the inquiry there "were issues around the involvement of the CFMEU" at the Office of Industrial Relations.

image.altText: CFMEU flags blowing in the wind

items.title: Adam was quoted $60,000 for his home insurance. His local council is taking action

children.content: Adam Osborne was quoted a staggering $60,000 for his home insurance this year. With others in the same boat, six Queensland councils are looking at creating their own insurance mutual to drive down premiums.

gallery.altText: man in blue workshirt walking along the river

thumbnail.altText: man in blue workshirt walking along the river

items.title: Dozens of workers with disability made redundant by award-winning charity

children.content: Help Enterprises made 41 positions redundant weeks after it was named Brisbane's social enterprise of the year.

image.altText: A close up of a man's hands in his lap.

items.title: Record entrants rev up for Rockynats festival despite fuel crisis

children.content: Amid the nation's ongoing fuel crunch, thousands of motoring enthusiasts from across the country roll up for Queensland's largest car and motorcycle festival.

image.altText: A woman standing next to a bright orange Torana car.

items.title: 2032 Olympic rowing 'will be in Rockhampton': Mander

children.content: The Queensland sports minister has once again emphasised that the Brisbane 2032 Games will be an Olympics for the whole state, saying there are no alternatives planned for the contentious proposed rowing venue in Rockhampton.

gallery.altText: Tim Mander and Andrew Liveris speak

thumbnail.altText: Tim Mander and Andrew Liveris speak
